title: approvals — HTTP middleware
description: Three middleware alii. `approve` is the LOAD-BEARING piece — every action decorated with `#[AsApprovableAction]` routes through it (design.md D-A5 rejected the trait approach because PHP mechanics don't support intercepting `__invoke()` after args have been resolved). `approvals.enforce_approver` sits in front of approve/reject endpoints as a deep-defense layer. `approvals.throttle_reminders` prevents template misconfiguration from spamming approvers.

middleware.description: The load-bearing middleware. Runs AFTER auth + tenant.resolve + permission gates. Intercepts routes whose action class carries `#[AsApprovableAction]`. Flow: (1) Read attribute via reflection on the resolved controller/action. (2) If no attribute → no-op pass-through. (3) Check scoped('approvals.executing') flag — if set to this instance_id, we're in the executor's replay path — pass through (prevents infinite loops). (4) Load approval_templates WHERE tenant_id=current AND application_id=current AND action_key=matched AND is_active=true. (5) Call action->approvalContext(...) to snapshot the context. (6) For each template ordered by priority ASC: evaluate template.when_expression via ExpressionLanguageAdapter against the snapshot — FIRST match wins. (7) If no template matches → pass through (no approval required for this action's current inputs). (8) Match found → ApprovalInstanceProvisioner creates approval_instance + requirements + resolves approvers → RETURNS 202 Accepted with { instance_id, status: 'pending', approvers[] } — the underlying action DOES NOT run yet. (9) When the flow later transitions to approved, ApprovalInstanceExecutor SETS the scoped flag + re-dispatches the action → this middleware sees the flag → passes through.
notes: The action's `approvalContext()` method MUST be pure — no DB writes, no side effects. It's called at least twice per approved flow (once at request time, once at execute time) and MUST return the same shape both times.

The scoped flag mechanism uses Laravel's `Context::scoped(fn () => ...)` API — flag is set in ApprovalInstanceExecutor's dispatch closure + auto-cleared on closure exit. Nested action calls inside the executed action DO NOT inherit the flag (Laravel's Context::scoped ends at the closure boundary), so nested approvals correctly re-trigger the middleware.
This middleware NEVER creates instances for GET/HEAD requests. Approval flows are for state-mutating actions only.

middleware.description: Attached to approve + reject endpoints. Validates the caller is in the target requirement's resolved_approvers[] BEFORE the policy check runs. Rejects with 403 approval.caller_not_approver + fires ApprovalSecurityViolation(violation_type='forged_decision') on failure. This is the FIRST of THREE independent enforcement layers for the caller_is_approver invariant (the other two: caller_is_approver validation rule on the FormRequest + ApprovalDecisionObserver's creating hook). Defense-in-depth against a policy misconfiguration opening a forgery seam.

Prevents template misconfiguration from spamming approvers via ad-hoc reminder-dispatch endpoints (e.g. admin 'send reminder now' button). Checks the (requirement, approver, current_hour) triple against a Redis counter — refuses when > 3 reminders sent this hour to the same approver-requirement pair. Refuses with 429 approval.reminder_rate_limit. The scheduled DispatchApprovalRemindersJob has its own composite-unique de-dup and doesn't hit this middleware; this covers only manual dispatches.
